title: How To Improve UX In Legacy Systems
description: Practical guidelines for driving UX impact in organizations with legacy systems and broken processes. Brought to you by Measuring UX Impact, **friendly video course on UX** and design patterns by Vitaly.

title: Identifying Necessary Transparency Moments In Agentic AI (Part 1)
description: Designing for agentic AI requires attention to both the system’s behavior and the transparency of its actions. Between the black box and the data dump lies a more thoughtful approach. Victor Yocco explores how to map decision points and reveal the right moments to build trust through clarity, not noise.

title: A Practical Guide To Design Principles
description: Design principles with references, examples, and methods for quick look-up. Brought to you by Design Patterns For AI Interfaces, **friendly video courses on UX** and design patterns by Vitaly.

title: Testing Font Scaling For Accessibility With Figma Variables
description: Accessibility works best when it blends into everyday design workflows. The goal isn’t a big transformation, but simple work processes that fit naturally into a team’s routine. With Figma variables, testing font size increases becomes part of the design flow itself, making accessibility feel almost inevitable rather than optional.

title: Modal vs. Separate Page: UX Decision Tree
description: How to choose between modals and pages, when to avoid modals, and how to determine the right level of interruption or navigation. Brought to you by Smart Interface Design Patterns, a **friendly video course on UX** and design patterns by Vitaly.

title: Anime vs. Marvel/DC: Designing Digital Products With Emotion In Flow
description: Design is about pacing and feelings as much as pixels and patterns. Alan Cohen explores Emotion in Flow and Emotion in Conflict, showing how anime like Dan Da Dan and superhero films like James Gunn’s Superman manage emotional shifts and translating those ideas into practical patterns for product design.

title: Persuasive Design: Ten Years Later
description: Many product teams still lean on usability improvements and isolated behavioral tweaks to address weak activation, drop-offs, and low retention – only to see results plateau or slip into shallow gamification. Anders Toxboe updates persuasive design for today’s reality, clarifying what has actually held up over the last decade.

title: Human Strategy In An AI-Accelerated Workflow
description: UX design is entering a new phase, with designers shifting from makers of outputs to directors of intent. AI can now generate wireframes, prototypes, and even design systems in minutes, but UX has never been only about creating interfaces. It’s about navigating ambiguity, advocating for humans in systems optimised for efficiency, and solving their problems through thoughtful design.

title: Now Shipping: Accessible UX Research, A New Smashing Book By Michele Williams
description: Our newest Smashing Book, “Accessible UX Research” by Michele Williams, is finally shipping worldwide — and we couldn’t be happier! This book is about research, but you’ll also learn about assistive technology, different types of disability, and how to build accessibility into the entire design process. This thoughtful book will get you thinking about ways to make your UX research more inclusive and thorough, no matter your budget or timeline. Jump to the book details or order your copy now.

title: Fresh Energy In March (2026 Wallpapers Edition)
description: Do you need a little inspiration boost? Well, then our new batch of desktop wallpapers is for you. Designed by the community for the community, the wallpapers in this collection are the perfect opportunity to get your desktop ready for spring — and, who knows, maybe they’ll spark some new ideas, too. Enjoy!

title: Say Cheese! Meet SmashingConf Amsterdam 🇳🇱
description: Meet our brand new conference for designers and UI engineers who love the web. That’s [SmashingConf Amsterdam](https://smashingconf.com/amsterdam-2026), taking place in the legendary Pathé Tuschinski, on April 13–16, 2026.

title: A Designer’s Guide To Eco-Friendly Interfaces
description: Every high-resolution hero image, autoplay video, and complex JavaScript animation carries a cost. Sustainable UX challenges the era of “unlimited pixels” and reframes performance as responsibility. In 2026, truly sophisticated design is defined not by how much it adds, but by how thoughtfully it reduces its footprint.

title: Designing A Streak System: The UX And Psychology Of Streaks
description: What makes streaks so powerful and addictive? To design them well, you need to understand how they align with human psychology. Victor Ayomipo breaks down the UX and design principles behind effective streak systems.

title: Building Digital Trust: An Empathy-Centred UX Framework For Mental Health Apps
description: Designing for mental health means designing for vulnerability. Empathy-Centred UX becomes not a “nice to have” but a fundamental design requirement. Here’s a practical framework for building trust-first mental health products.

title: Designing For Agentic AI: Practical UX Patterns For Control, Consent, And Accountability
description: Autonomy is an output of a technical system. Trustworthiness is an output of a design process. Here are concrete design patterns, operational frameworks, and organizational practices for building agentic systems that are not only powerful but also transparent, controllable, and trustworthy.

title: Combobox vs. Multiselect vs. Listbox: How To Choose The Right One
description: Combobox vs. Multi-Select vs. Listbox vs. Dual Listbox? How they are different, what purpose they serve, and how to choose the right one. Brought to you by Design Patterns For AI Interfaces, **friendly video courses on UX** and design patterns by Vitaly.

title: Short Month, Big Ideas (February 2026 Wallpapers Edition)
description: Let’s make the most of the shortest month of the year with a new collection of desktop wallpapers that are sure to bring a smile to your face — and maybe spark your creativity, too. All of them were designed with love by the community for the community and can be downloaded for free. Happy February!

title: Beyond Generative: The Rise Of Agentic AI And User-Centric Design
description: Developing effective agentic AI requires a new research playbook. When systems plan, decide, and act on our behalf, UX moves beyond usability testing into the realm of trust, consent, and accountability. Victor Yocco outlines the research methods needed to design agentic AI systems responsibly.

title: Rethinking “Pixel Perfect” Web Design
description: Amit Sheen takes a hard look at the “Pixel Perfect” legacy concept, explaining why it’s failing us and redefining what “perfection” actually looks like in a multi-device, fluid world.

title: Smashing Animations Part 8: Theming Animations Using CSS Relative Colour
description: CSS relative colour values are now widely supported. In this article, pioneering author and web designer [Andy Clarke](https://stuffandnonsense.co.uk/) shares practical techniques for using them to theme and animate SVG graphics.

title: UX And Product Designer’s Career Paths In 2026
description: How to shape your career path for 2026, with decision trees for designers and a UX skills self-assessment matrix. The only limits for tomorrow are the doubts we have today. Brought to you by Smart Interface Design Patterns, a **friendly video course on UX** and design patterns by Vitaly.

title: Penpot Is Experimenting With MCP Servers For AI-Powered Design Workflows
description: [Penpot](https://penpot.app/?utm_source=SmashingMagazine&utm_medium=Article&utm_campaign=MCPserver) is experimenting with MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers, which could lead to designers and developers being able to perform tasks in Penpot using AI that’s able to understand and interact with Penpot design files. Daniel Schwarz explains how [Penpot MCP](https://github.com/penpot/penpot-mcp) servers work, what they could mean for creating and managing designs in Penpot, and what you can do to h

title: How To Design For (And With) Deaf People
description: Practical UX guidelines to keep in mind for 466 million people who experience hearing loss. More design patterns in Smart Interface Design Patterns, a **friendly video course on UX** and design patterns by Vitaly.

title: How To Measure The Impact Of Features
description: Meet TARS — a simple, repeatable, and meaningful UX metric designed specifically to track the performance of product features. Upcoming part of the Measure UX & Design Impact (use the code 🎟 IMPACT to save 20% off today).

title: Smashing Animations Part 7: Recreating Toon Text With CSS And SVG
description: In this article, pioneering author and web designer [Andy Clarke](https://stuffandnonsense.co.uk) shows his techniques for creating [Toon Text titles](https://stuffandnonsense.co.uk/toon-text/index.html) using modern CSS and SVG.

title: Renovation Design of the Library, Chengdu Shude Experimental Middle School / Modum Atelier
description: Design Approach: Generating a Strategic Prototype - The renovation of the library at Chengdu Shude Experimental Middle School faces the typical challenges of updating an old school building. Located on the ground floor of a building constructed in the 1970s–80s, the structure has undergone multiple renovations and ownership changes, leaving its original design unrecorded. Within this orthogonal grid space, the school requires not only reading functions but also multi-purpose uses including meeti

title: Nonhyun 169  / See Architects
description: Located at the intersection of a lively commercial street and a modest residential alley in Seoul's Gangnam district, the site presented a dual character—active and visible yet bound by zoning regulations that restrict the building height to four stories with less FAR than typical commercial areas. This posed a design challenge: how to create a commercial presence that stands out without exceeding the area's formal constraints.

title: Gosford Regional Library / Lahznimmo Architects
description: The Gosford Regional Library has been designed to generate a civic presence within the Civic Heart of Gosford, directly addressing the public space of Kibble Park. Kibble Park itself serves the symbolic role of 'Town Square', and the new Library will, in turn, symbolically act as a 'Town Hall'.

title: PUKKUN Residence / REIMS 502
description: Suburban tropical residence for a family from Cancún that, through multiple design strategies, incorporates anextensive architectural program and integrates with a sensitized “sense of place” within its immediate context

title: Josephine Baker Dance Hall / Onze04 Architecture
description: The local authority wants to build a new cultural and sports facility that will be able to host regional-level sports competitions. The chosen location is next to two existing facilities: the Pierre de Coubertin sports complex and the André Blot cultural centre. Other facilities are close by but disconnected from these: the stadium, tennis courts, bowling green, and BMX track.

title: Buildner Launches Unbuilt Award 2026 With €100K in Awards and Announces 2nd Edition Winners
description: Buildner has launched the Unbuilt Award 2026, the third edition of its annual competition, offering a €100,000 prize fund. At the same time, the results of the Unbuilt Award 2025 have been announced, marking the second competition in a series that celebrates architectural designs that have yet to be realized. The initiative provides a global platform for architects and designers to showcase their most compelling unbuilt projects—whether conceptual, published, unpublished, or fully developed.

title: Reused Shipping Containers Form A Flexible Workplace in Spain
description: Designed by Impepinable Studio, a workplace formed of reused shipping containers provides flexibility for an agro-industrial company in Spain.

title: An Argument for Interior Design with Neuroaesthetics in Mind
description: John Hopkins University's International Arts + Mind Lab publishes a report calling for the development of a new interdisciplinary Neuroarchitecture sector.

title: IKEA Emerges a Cheeky Disruptor in the Collectible Space with GREJSIMOJS
description: GREJSIMOJS turns the collectible design conversation on its head, swapping distance and decorum for tactile joy, life-sized whimsy, and a riot of rebel color.

title: 丝路精神—西部国际设计双年展
competition_type: 视觉 / 空间 / 非遗
description: 在西安等地举办的西部设计双年展及相关征集，侧重丝绸之路文化与设计教育交流。

title: 中国服务设计大会优秀案例征集
description: 服贸会期间中国服务设计大会配套的优秀案例、机构与导师征集；细则见 UXPA 中国当年通知。

title: 全国绿色建筑设计竞赛（深绿设）
competition_type: 绿色建筑 / 可持续建筑
description: 面向建筑院校与从业者的绿色建筑设计征集，按学生年级与职业分组别；届次以主办方公布为准。
organizer: 深圳市深绿建筑设计有限公司等

title: 全国大学生海洋文化创意设计大赛
competition_type: 海洋主题 / 视觉 / 产品
description: 以海洋文化与科普为主题的公益设计赛，常设平面、产品、数媒与 AIGC 等赛道。

title: 全国大学生红色旅游创意策划大赛
competition_type: 文旅策划 / 视觉 / 空间
description: 由文化和旅游部资源开发司等单位指导的红色文旅线路、IP 与数字作品类竞赛。
organizer: 北京第二外国语学院等承办单位

title: Red Dot Award: Brands & Communication Design
description: 红点奖中面向品牌、包装、视觉传达与广告等方向的竞赛单元。
prize: 红点品牌与传达设计奖

title: Red Dot Award: Design Concept
description: 红点奖中面向概念、原型与尚未量产方案的设计竞赛单元。

title: "Design isn’t something you look at, it’s something you live in": 5 questions with Wybe Magermans
description: WMH&I's director of strategy & growth discusses the flaws of design pretending to be strategy and the future of the creative industry.

title: Songtsm Travel Hotel Jiuzhai / Thinking Design
description: On a 3,000-meter-high ridge in Jiuzhaigou lies a micro-village of just eight wooden cabins—China's smallest administrative village. It appears pinned against the sky by the wind, coexisting with clouds, fir trees, and rocky terrain. Now, we are tasked with transforming this unique site into a resort. Design here is not about "renovation," but about "recalling": recalling the memories sealed within the land, the breath hidden in the mountain forests and timber, and the traces of time buried in th

title: University of Limerick Student Center / Carr Cotter & Naessens
description: The new Student Center for the University of Limerick, which is located in a significant site at the gateway to the campus, was generated by a vision for a place that is at the very heart of university life, transforming the social experience of the growing student population by providing social spaces, places for encounter and exchange of information, a center to support both students and alumni-a home from home, a place on campus that students can call their own. The project was first envisage

title: The Babel Community - Coliving Residence  / D'HOUNDT+BAJART Architectes&associés
description: Located in the heart of a Lille business district developed around forty years ago, the Babel Community coliving residence is part of a broader process of urban and environmental regeneration. The project involves the transformation of a site formerly occupied by obsolete and energy-inefficient office buildings. In their place now stands a 304-room hospitality residence complemented by a wide range of services and shared amenities, conceived as a new living environment within an area historicall

title: A Modular Form Where Geometry Quietly Becomes Furniture
description: A Modular Form Where Geometry Quietly Becomes FurnitureFractal 9 began not as a formal design project but as a response to a simple, everyday need. The goal was to make use of...

title: This minimal AI camera blends into the wild while quietly tracking wildlife
description: This minimal AI camera blends into the wild while quietly tracking wildlifeThere’s a quiet shift happening in how we observe wildlife. It’s no longer about being physically present with bulky gear and long lenses, but about...

title: Bugatti Type Sigma Concept Ditches the Chiron’s Maximalist Design for Pure Sculptural Form
description: Bugatti Type Sigma Concept Ditches the Chiron’s Maximalist Design for Pure Sculptural FormBugatti built the Type 57SC Atlantic in the 1930s using a technique called riveted construction, where aluminum panels were joined with raised seams that became...

title: Old Tape, New Tricks: Maxell’s Cassette Player Goes Wireless
description: Old Tape, New Tricks: Maxell’s Cassette Player Goes WirelessEvery few years, the tech industry digs up something from the past, slaps a USB-C port on it, and calls it innovation. Sometimes it works....

title: 5 Best Design Tools That Take You From the First Sketch to the Finished Prototype
description: 5 Best Design Tools That Take You From the First Sketch to the Finished PrototypeThe design process has always lived between two worlds: the rough immediacy of a hand-drawn line and the precision of a finished object you can...

title: 2026 ArchDaily 中国年度建筑大奖，决选名单公布
description: 随着过去两周的激烈角逐，ArchDaily 的读者们已经从 455 个项目中票选出 ArchDaily 2026 中国年度建筑大奖的前 15 名。在提名阶段，我们共同见证了这个大奖竞选对建筑发展的积极作用。这些进入决选名单的建筑项目无疑是最能给我们的读者带去灵感，也同样反映出建筑社会的发展趋势。

title: 成都树德中学东马棚校区图书馆改造 / 衡建筑
description: 设计破题：一种策略原型的生成成都树德实验中学图书室的改造，面临着典型的老旧校舍更新困境。项目位于一栋建于上世纪七八十年代的老建筑底层，历经修缮与产权变更，原始结构设计不可考。在这个正交网格的空间内，校方希望满足阅读功能之余，还能兼顾会议、多功能教学、教师备课以及展览等复合需求。

title: PETÉN 公寓 / REIMS 502
description: 在墨西哥城的韦尔蒂斯 - 纳瓦尔特（Vértiz-Narvarte）社区，20 世纪 50 年代的建筑展现出了一种现代建筑风格，其特点为线条简洁、几何造型大胆以及立面简约。在此背景下，1956 年，建筑师兼土木工程师恩里克·埃尔南德斯·卡马雷纳（Enrique Hernández Camarena）为工薪阶层居民设计建造了一座多户住宅楼。

title: 诺斯科特之家 / LLDS
description: 针对墨尔本现有的城市环境，LLDS 重新构思了维多利亚式联排住宅的建筑类型，在澳大利亚墨尔本打造了一座紧凑的内城住宅。该住宅坐落于一块东西向的狭长地块上，地块长 22 米，宽 4.6 米。设计的主要举措是将地面抬高，形成屋顶花园，以解决花园空间不足的问题。棕色的屋顶在城市环境中支持着当地的生态。自由形式的木质结构下方是一个大厅般的房间，设有厨房、餐厅和入口门廊，让人联想起周边的大型工厂阁楼和维多利亚式教堂礼堂。高度纹理化的混凝土内墙提供了热质量，并通过减少平行边界墙引起的颤动回声效应，改善了餐厅的声学效果。

title: 斯杰诺普城堡遗址更新 / Tengbom
description: 罗克森湖（Lake Roxen）西侧、林雪平市郊矗立着斯杰诺普城堡（Stjärnorp Castle）遗址，该城堡最初建于 1654 年至 1662 年间，其设计者极有可能是老尼科德默斯·特辛（Nicodemus Tessin the Elder）。这座庄园包括一栋四层石质主楼，主楼设有一座面向西边公园的楼梯塔，以及两座朝向东边罗克森湖的侧翼建筑。1697 年，埃里克·达尔伯格（Erik Dahlbergh）所著的《古与今之瑞典》（Suecia Antiqua et Hodierna）一书中描绘了这座城堡。

title: 建筑师/设计师如何重新定义商业空间？
description: 在当代建筑领域，商业空间已不再仅仅是销售场所；它们已成为身份、形象与体验交汇的舞台。店铺、展厅以及品牌室内空间常常充当实验室的角色，建筑师在此试验形式、材质与光线，将企业叙事转化为空间体验。在此背景下，建筑师化身为欲望的塑造者，营造出引导感知、唤起情感并潜移默化影响行为的空间氛围。这一角色揭示了设计与资本主义之间错综复杂的交集：这些空间不仅售卖产品，更售卖憧憬、生活方式与文化内涵。通过将商业转化为一场建筑表演，这些项目促使我们反思：在可见性与形象变得与功能同样至关重要的世界里，建筑学科如何权衡并发挥其影响力。

title: HVM 玛雅 / Perkins&Will
description: 在奢华建筑常常屈服于全球化复制的时代，由 Perkins&Will 圣保罗分公司在大坎普（MS州）打造的新项目，成为了一个转折点。

title: 凯伊公寓 / Grzywinski+Pons
description: 凯伊大楼（Kaye Residences）是位于西雅图贝尔敦（Belltown）社区的一栋崭新的 31 层建筑，内含 324 套公寓，底层、七层和三十二层设有酒店级配套设施，夹层设有共享办公空间，底层还设有商业空间。Grzywinski+Pons 事务所为客户斯堪斯卡（Skanska）公司设计了这栋建筑及其所有内部空间，还设计了大部分家具。

title: Kaloki Nyamai 工作室 / Adjaye Associates
description: “卡伦（Karen）地区的景观静谧而厚重，工作室的设计理念是轻盈地融入这种环境。我们的目标是打造一个既能让人沉思冥想，又能进行创作的空间，在这里，材料、光线和结构都以一种克制的姿态相互交融。建筑借鉴了非洲的典型形态，以此思考永恒性、气候与记忆。这是一个创作过程与环境缓慢对话、徐徐展开的地方。” —— David Adjaye

title: 南海大沥永平仓改造设计 / 竖梁社
description: 南海大沥曾是一座典型的珠三角产业型城镇，奔跑在快速发展的高速公路上，忽视了对有温度的城市多元公共生活的关注。随着南海区千灯湖城市轴线北延，这座城市正式开启从工业向宜居的转型，在密集城区中挖掘更多的公共空间，打造更多的公园。本案坐落于花地河畔，沿河一线，地理位置优越，从属于盐步老龙 1432 片区。该区域历史上曾经是“省佛通衢”的交通要道。如同盐步直街、盐步酒厂、三圈火柴厂等一系列承载着盐步商贸文化的老地标，永平仓也因商贸往来、航运交通而生。随着时代的发展，它逐渐失去旧有功能，闲置废弃，却占据了城市滨水最优质的景观活动空间。

title: 今日设计工作室 / studio edwards
description: 今日设计工作室坐落于库林民族（Kulin Nation）中沃伦迪杰里 / 沃伊沃伦贡（Wurundjeri / Woiwurrung）族人的传统土地上，位于充满活力的科林伍德社区内的一栋 12 层办公楼中。在这片空间里，一块 900 平方米的空白画布经过精心雕琢，旨在为数字机构今日设计（Today Design）营造一个促进协作与创新的氛围。它犹如一座包容性的灯塔，热情欢迎客户、合作伙伴及团队成员的到来。该项目的一大显著特点是对可持续发展的坚定承诺。

title: 红色阁楼  / DUHA Architects
description: 红色阁楼是一座轻量化、可逆的接待项目，坐落于越南顺化香江受保护的滨河走廊地带，当地严格的法规旨在保护自然景观。在此背景下，该项目将这些限制条件视为其建筑特色的基石。这座阁楼专为一对年轻夫妇打造，既是他们的第二居所，也是一处精品度假胜地，采用了可组装、拆卸并恢复原状的可逆系统，将对场地的长期影响降至最低。

title: 筑造之路：圣地亚哥朝圣之路上的朝圣之旅与建筑序列
description: 朝圣是人类最古老且最持久的文化习俗之一，是人类跨越地理与宗教界限，探寻生命意义的具象化表达。尽管朝圣传统上与正式的信仰体系紧密相连，但近几十年来，其定义已有所拓展，反映出人们对何为神圣、何处可寻生命意义的新认知。这一转变揭示了一个根本性的问题：在空间中穿行，始终是人们构建有意义体验的核心方式。然而，如今大多数建筑环境的设计，都是为了让人从公路、交通走廊、机场和优化后的城市中心快速抵达。而圣地亚哥朝圣之路则是对这一现状的持久反驳。它是一种分布式建筑，历经数个世纪精雕细琢，至今仍是围绕移动的人体而组织设计的典范。

title: Apartment A / heros
description: 在一座 20 世纪 70 年代的建筑顶层，一套复式公寓的改造为人们带来了一种全新的高空居住体验。这套通透的公寓慷慨地敞向两座绿植环绕的露台，这些露台如同真正的户外延伸空间，邀请人们在城市中心享受放松时光。该项目以简洁的线条和开阔的空间，受建筑极简主义和日本设计理念的启发，重新诠释了空间美学。

title: 彬妈糖水 / 治木设计
description: 九龙湖的水色与山形，给了这个空间最初的情绪底色。我们以"归巢"为设计母题，将岭南糖水铺里代代相传的烟火气，不动声色地揉进阿那亚特有的度假感与归属感之中。

title: 奥伯阿姆泰大街历史博物馆 / wulf architekten
description: 位于奥伯阿姆泰斯特（Oberamteistraße）大街 28-32 号的历史悠久的房屋群，以及 1972 年被拆除的奥伯阿姆泰斯特大街 34 号“石头屋”的残存地下室，是罗伊特林根市最古老、最引人入胜的建筑之一。
